memory_items.title: Safe Row Deletion Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When deleting multiple rows, collect indices first then delete in reverse order
memory_items.generalization: For multi-row deletions, build a list of target row indices before modifying the worksheet, then process deletions from highest to lowest row number to prevent index shifting errors

memory_items.title: Result Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Always validate changes by reloading and rechecking conditions
memory_items.generalization: After writing modifications, reload the saved workbook independently and programmatically verify that required conditions hold across affected data ranges

memory_items.title: Robust Value Conversion
memory_items.lesson: Use try/except when converting cell values to numeric types
memory_items.generalization: Wrap type conversions in exception handlers to gracefully skip empty cells, formulas, or incompatible data types without crashing the entire operation

memory_items.title: Protected Header Boundary
memory_items.lesson: Adjust iteration range to preserve header rows during bulk operations
memory_items.generalization: When iterating over worksheet rows, set lower bounds above the header row to avoid accidental modification or deletion of structure-defining content

edits.content:
## Multi-Row Deletion Best Practices

### Safe Index Collection Strategy
When deleting multiple rows based on conditions:
1. Collect all target row indices in a separate list during initial scan
2. Process deletions from highest to lowest row number (reverse order)
3. This prevents row index shifting that causes incorrect deletions or skipped rows

### Post-Modification Validation
Always implement verification logic after saving changes:
1. Reload the saved workbook as a new instance
2. Programmatically verify all conditions now meet requirements
3. Log or return verification status for debugging and confidence

### Defensive Data Type Handling
When comparing cell values to thresholds:
1. Check for None/null values before conversion
2. Wrap numeric conversions in try/except blocks
3. Skip cells with unconvertible types rather than crashing

### Header Protection
When iterating through data rows:
1. Set iteration start/end bounds to exclude header row (typically row 1)
2. Use explicit row numbers rather than implicit assumptions
3. Document which rows are considered data vs. structure in comments

### Resource Cleanup
Ensure proper resource management:
1. Explicitly close workbook objects after operations complete
2. Use context managers where available
3. Avoid leaving file handles open between operations
